steps.title: Coalesce and launch the Experiential Learning Community
steps.summary: Build on work already under way through the July-August and September-October planning windows to bring together the founding experiential learning community, clarify its initial priorities and deliver the confirmed September 2026 launch under interim institutional leadership.

outputs.title: Experiential Learning Community launch
outputs.summary: A September launch with a founding membership, initial priorities and a clear rhythm for continued participation.

steps.title: Develop and establish the governance, resourcing and faculty support model
steps.summary: From September to December 2026, develop and establish the operating model, including interim and funded convenorship, community support, funded projects, accountability and the indicative resource envelope. Work with faculties to agree the principles through which future community convenors can receive release, workload allocation or backfill support, including central and faculty contributions, eligibility, duration and review. Establish the Innovation Committee by December with an agreed remit, membership and authority to govern the communities, convenorship and funded activity.

outputs.title: Operational Innovation Committee
outputs.summary: A governing committee with an agreed remit, membership and authority to steward the communities, convenorship and funded activity.

outputs.title: Governance and resourcing model
outputs.summary: An agreed model covering convenorship, community support, funded projects, accountability and the indicative resource envelope.

outputs.title: Agreed release and backfill principles
outputs.summary: A sufficiently clear and equitable institutional offer to support the first funded convenor and community-project calls.

steps.title: Confirm interim convenors and integrate the remaining communities
steps.summary: In November and December 2026, identify existing institutional leaders to act as interim, unremitted convenors and use their leadership to bring the AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities into the shared institutional frame.

outputs.title: Three communities operating in a common frame
outputs.summary: Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education each have interim leadership, a founding constituency and an initial programme of activity.

steps.title: Run the first funded convenor and community-project calls
steps.summary: From January to April 2027, run coordinated calls for funded Community Convenors and community-aligned projects. Complete assessment and selection by April so that successful convenors, faculty release and backfill arrangements, project leads and funding can be confirmed and mobilised for a September start.

outputs.title: Completed institutional calls
outputs.summary: Convenors and projects selected through clear eligibility, support, assessment and decision arrangements.

outputs.title: Selected funded leadership and project portfolio
outputs.summary: Successful convenors and projects identified for appointment and commencement in September 2027.

steps.title: Build the first three communities and establish their operating conventions
steps.summary: From January to June 2027, develop the Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities under interim leadership into active and recognisable communities of practice. Through their early work, establish and test shared conventions for convenorship, participation, communication, collaborative inquiry, student and partner involvement, funded activity, decision-making and routes into institutional influence.

outputs.title: Three established communities with shared operating conventions
outputs.summary: Three communities with interim leadership, sustained participation and credible programmes of activity, alongside an agreed and tested set of conventions to guide future communities of practice.

steps.title: Confirm appointments and mobilise the first funded cycle
steps.summary: From May to August 2027, confirm the selected convenors and projects, finalise faculty release, workload allocation, backfill, budgets and delivery arrangements, and manage the handover from interim leadership. Prepare the three communities, funded convenors and project teams to begin the first funded operating cycle in September.

outputs.title: Confirmed funded appointments and projects
outputs.summary: Convenors, project leads, budgets, workload arrangements and expectations confirmed for September commencement.

outputs.title: First funded cycle ready to operate
outputs.summary: The three communities and their funded activity are mobilised for the 2027-28 academic year.

steps.title: Review the initial mobilisation, agree the expansion portfolio and launch the 2028-29 calls
steps.summary: During August and September 2027, use the quieter period after the communities' initial activity falls away in July to review their establishment, participation, governance, interim convenorship, faculty support, project mechanisms and emerging institutional influence. Refine the model, agree the three additional strategic communities to be developed for 2028-29 and immediately launch the associated convenor and community-project calls.

outputs.title: Initial mobilisation review
outputs.summary: An evidence-led assessment of whether the first three communities and their shared operating model have been successfully established.

outputs.title: Approved six-community portfolio
outputs.summary: Three additional strategic communities agreed for development and funded operation in the 2028-29 academic year.

outputs.title: Open 2028-29 convenor and project calls
outputs.summary: Calls launched immediately for renewed and new convenors and community-aligned projects across the approved six-community portfolio.

steps.title: Operate the first funded three-community cycle
steps.summary: Operate the Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities through the September 2027-June 2028 academic-year cycle. Support their funded convenors, programmes, projects, participation and routes into institutional influence while gathering consistent evidence of activity, outcomes and resource requirements.

outputs.title: First funded operating cycle
outputs.summary: Three communities complete a coherent September-June cycle with funded leadership, visible activity and evidence of their contribution.

steps.title: Complete selection for the expanded portfolio
steps.summary: From September to December 2027, assess the 2028-29 convenor and community-project calls launched through the initial mobilisation review. Complete selection before Christmas, covering renewals, new appointments and the three additional communities, so that the expanded portfolio can move directly into mobilisation from January.

outputs.title: Selected 2028-29 convenors and projects
outputs.summary: Renewed and new convenors and a funded project portfolio selected for the six-community operating cycle beginning in September 2028.

steps.title: Mobilise the expanded six-community portfolio
steps.summary: From January to August 2028, confirm appointments, faculty release, workload allocation, backfill, budgets and project delivery arrangements. Establish the three additional communities, build their founding memberships and priorities, and prepare all six communities, convenors and funded projects to begin the next operating cycle together in September.

outputs.title: Expanded portfolio ready to operate
outputs.summary: All six communities, their funded convenors and their selected projects are mobilised for September 2028.

steps.title: Review the first funded operating cycle, refine the model and launch the 2029-30 calls
steps.summary: During August and September 2028, review the completed September-June operating cycle. Assess participation, activity, impact, governance, convenorship, faculty release and backfill, funding, project mechanisms and institutional influence, make final refinements for the expanded portfolio and immediately launch the 2029-30 convenor and community-project calls.

outputs.title: First funded-cycle impact and operating-model review
outputs.summary: An assessment of the first funded cycle with agreed refinements to governance, resourcing and operating arrangements for the expanded portfolio.

outputs.title: Open 2029-30 convenor and project calls
outputs.summary: Calls launched immediately for the next annual portfolio of convenors and community-aligned projects.

steps.title: Operate the six-community portfolio
steps.summary: Operate all six strategic communities through the September 2028-June 2029 academic-year cycle, supporting their funded convenors, programmes, projects, institutional influence and cross-community learning while gathering consistent evidence of participation, outcomes and resource requirements.

outputs.title: Full six-community operating cycle
outputs.summary: Six communities complete a coherent September-June cycle, including the contribution of their funded projects.

steps.title: Complete 2029-30 selection and develop the BAU settlement
steps.summary: From September to December 2028, assess the 2029-30 convenor and community-project calls and complete selection before Christmas for a September start. In parallel, develop the proposed permanent institutional ownership, governance route, budget holding, recurrent resource settlement and annual planning arrangements required for business as usual.

outputs.title: Selected 2029-30 portfolio
outputs.summary: Convenors and projects selected for the operating cycle beginning in September 2029.

outputs.title: Proposed BAU resource and governance settlement
outputs.summary: A developed proposal for permanent ownership, governance, recurrent resources and the continuing annual commissioning rhythm.

steps.title: Mobilise the 2029-30 portfolio and prepare the institutional handover
steps.summary: From January to August 2029, confirm appointments, faculty release, backfill, budgets and project delivery arrangements for the 2029-30 portfolio. Prepare the transfer of governance, reporting, operational support, budgets and accountability from mobilisation to the proposed permanent institutional owner.

outputs.title: Portfolio and handover ready for September
outputs.summary: The next annual portfolio is mobilised and the operational, governance and resource arrangements are ready to transfer into business as usual.

steps.title: Review the six-community cycle and confirm the permanent model
steps.summary: During August and September 2029, review the completed six-community operating cycle, assess impact and sustainability, determine which communities should continue or evolve, and approve the permanent institutional owner, governance arrangements, recurrent resource settlement and annual planning cycle.

outputs.title: Six-community impact and sustainability review
outputs.summary: A completed assessment of the expanded portfolio and its contribution after a full operating cycle.

outputs.title: Approved permanent institutional model
outputs.summary: Permanent ownership, governance, recurrent resources and the annual commissioning and operating rhythm confirmed.

steps.title: Transition to business as usual
steps.summary: In September 2029, complete the transfer to the permanent institutional owner so that Strategic Communities of Practice continue through the established rhythm of September-June operation, August-September review and call launch, September-December selection and January-August mobilisation.

outputs.title: Business-as-usual transition complete
outputs.summary: Strategic Communities of Practice operate through permanent ownership, governance, recurrent resources and an established annual planning cycle from September 2029.
